_meta.purpose: Curated SSOT for /miscarriage/ topic hub. Comparison cards (mainstream workup vs RRM evaluation) and treatable causes reference cards rendered as Astro card grids.
_meta.source: Library citations, ASRM 2012 RPL committee opinion (reaffirmed 2020, updated 2026), Kuroda 2021, Cicinelli 2013, Boyle 2024 (estradiol/DHEA), Stanford 2008, Tham 2012, Boyle 2010, Negro 2010, McQueen 2019, Santulli 2016, Balasch 1986.
_meta.card_notes: Each card field is an HTML fragment with inline citation hyperlinks to library records. Render with Astro set:html. Inline citations are MANDATORY per editorial policy.

workup_comparison.name: Standard RPL Workup
workup_comparison.subtitle: What most practices offer after two to three losses
workup_comparison.threshold_html: Guideline threshold is two losses, but practice patterns commonly defer to three or more before initiating systematic evaluation.
workup_comparison.tests_included_html:
• Antiphospholipid antibody panel
  • Parental karyotype
  • Uterine anatomy (2D ultrasound or SHG)
  • Thyroid function (TSH)
workup_comparison.tests_typically_excluded_html:
• Endometrial biopsy with CD138 for chronic endometritis
  • Sperm DNA fragmentation
  • Cycle-timed progesterone (luteal phase)
  • Full thyroid panel (free T4, TPO antibodies)
  • Androgen panel (DHEA-S, testosterone)
  • Endometriosis evaluation
workup_comparison.outcome_when_nothing_found_html: Labeled "unexplained recurrent pregnancy loss." Surveillance offered. No targeted treatment.
workup_comparison.rrm_stance: Incomplete. The most prevalent treatable causes are not routinely tested.

workup_comparison.name: Cause-Based RPL Evaluation
workup_comparison.subtitle: RRM-trained clinician approach: evaluate after first or second loss, both partners
workup_comparison.threshold_html: Begins after first loss when risk factors present; standard after two losses regardless of age.
workup_comparison.tests_included_html:
• Cycle charting (FABM) as foundational data layer
  • Antiphospholipid antibody panel
  • Parental karyotype when indicated
  • 3D TVUS or sonohysterogram
  • Full thyroid panel: TSH, free T4, TPO antibodies
  • Cycle-timed progesterone levels
  • Androgen panel: DHEA-S, testosterone when indicated
  • Prolactin
  • Glucose / fasting insulin if PCOS suspected
  • Endometrial biopsy with CD138 immunohistochemistry for CE
  • Endometrial culture
  • Partner semen analysis with sperm DNA fragmentation
  • Hysteroscopy when imaging is suggestive
workup_comparison.tests_typically_excluded_html: None of the above are excluded by default. Workup is individualized but the differential is thorough.
workup_comparison.outcome_when_nothing_found_html: Genuine 'nothing found' is rare when the full panel is run. When it occurs, the couple is counseled on age-related factors and monitoring in subsequent pregnancies.
workup_comparison.rrm_stance: Endorsed as the standard. Most 'unexplained' RPL has an identifiable cause when the right questions are asked.

treatable_causes.name: Chronic Endometritis
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: 57.8% of women with unexplained recurrent miscarriage in a study of 360 women. Cicinelli et al. 2013.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: Endometrial biopsy with CD138 immunohistochemistry staining. Endometrial culture. Hysteroscopy for direct visualization.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: Targeted antibiotic therapy based on culture results. Physician-directed.
treatable_causes.cross_link_html: See the chronic endometritis guide for a full account.

treatable_causes.name: Luteal Phase Deficiency
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: Documented in the RPL literature since Horta et al. 1977. Found in 19 of 60 recurrent abortion patients in a cohort study, significantly higher than fertile controls. Balasch et al. 1986. Charting-visible. Frequently undetected without cycle data.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: Cycle charting (FABM) to document luteal phase length. Cycle-timed progesterone levels in the mid-luteal phase.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: Progesterone support during luteal phase and early pregnancy when deficiency is documented. Timing relative to implantation is clinically significant. Physician-directed.

treatable_causes.name: Hypoandrogenemia / Low DHEA-S
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: Miscarriage rate 45.5% in low-estradiol women without supplementation vs 17.5% in DHEA-supplemented group (p=0.038). Boyle et al. 2024.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: Androgen panel: DHEA-S, testosterone. Not included in standard RPL workup.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: DHEA supplementation to restore estradiol levels when hypoandrogenemia is documented. Physician-directed.

treatable_causes.name: Thyroid Dysfunction
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: Subclinical hypothyroidism (TSH 2.5-5.0) associated with 6.1% pregnancy loss vs 3.6% in women with TSH below 2.5 in a prospective study of 4,123 women. Negro et al. 2010.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: Full thyroid panel: TSH, free T4, TPO antibodies. TSH alone is insufficient.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: Thyroid optimization under physician supervision when indicated.
treatable_causes.on_standard_rpl_panel: TSH often yes; free T4 and TPO antibodies often no

treatable_causes.name: Antiphospholipid Syndrome
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: One of the best-documented treatable causes. Present in a clinically significant minority of RPL cases. Requires two positive tests at least 12 weeks apart for diagnosis. Rai et al. 1997.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: Lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin IgG/IgM, anti-beta-2 glycoprotein-I IgG/IgM. Two tests required.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: Anticoagulation during pregnancy. Physician-directed. No protocol details on public pages.

treatable_causes.name: Sperm DNA Fragmentation
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: Significantly elevated in male partners of women with RPL. Mean difference 11.91 vs fertile controls (95% CI 4.97-18.86) in a meta-analysis of 13 studies. McQueen et al. 2019.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: Sperm DNA fragmentation test (DFI). Not included in standard semen analysis.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: Evaluation by reproductive urologist. Lifestyle and antioxidant intervention where modifiable. Physician-directed.

treatable_causes.name: Anatomic Factors
treatable_causes.prevalence_in_rpl_html: Uterine septum is the anatomic anomaly most consistently linked to recurrent miscarriage in observational data, per Wang et al. 2019. Submucosal fibroids, polyps, and adhesions are less common but warrant evaluation.
treatable_causes.diagnosis_html: 3D transvaginal ultrasound or sonohysterogram as first-line. Hysteroscopy as gold standard for direct visualization and treatment.
treatable_causes.treatment_html: Hysteroscopic correction when individually indicated. Septum resection is an option but randomized data (TRUST RCT, Rikken et al. 2021) have not demonstrated a definitive benefit over expectant management; decision is individualized. Surgeon-directed.
treatable_causes.on_standard_rpl_panel: Pelvic ultrasound often yes; 3D TVUS or SHG variable
